other_observers: Brenda Wright (photographer), George Steele (first observer), Kathey Graaff and others.
description: Small to medium size raptor. George Steele called us and said he had a Mississippe Kite in his yard. Of course, I said "no way". Upon arriving at his house a hawk was seen perched in a tree in his back yard. Immediately, I knew it was a kite. The bird was very light gray, lighter around the head than the rest of the body. The bill was small and the reddish eye was surrounded by a black mask.  A few white spots on the back and folded wings and a couple of rows of white spots on the tail which suggest the kite was a first summer bird. We never saw the bird fly but it was reported flying around Norwood catching grasshoppers for the next few days and eating them on the wing.
